creator: nimaje+fbz@bureaucracy.de
text:
I don't see how that comment (or anything related to that issue) shows that "they care a shit about compatibility". That symbol isn't (and wasn't) part of any header or in any documentation, so it isn't part of public API and subject to change at any time.

Yes, it is unfortunate, that the incorrect usage of the symbol was found because of that change (but the workaround needed for windows should have been clear enought already).
